source.section: Steps for the EWI sampling method (Step 1-2)

steps.label: Set out safety equipment and park the vehicle to avoid sample contamination

constraints.text: Upon arrival at the field site, set out safety equipment such as traffic cones and signs.
Park vehicle in a location and direction so as to prevent sample contamination from vehicle emissions.

steps.label: Assemble the sampling equipment and set up a clean work space

constraints.text: Organic compounds: Select equipment with fluorocarbon polymer, glass, or metal components if components will directly contact samples to be analyzed for organic compounds.
Do not use plastics other than fluorocarbon polymers for organic-compound sampling.

Inorganic constituents: Select equipment with components made of fluorocarbon polymer or other relatively inert and uncolored plastics or glass if components will directly contact samples to be analyzed for inorganic constituents.
Do not use metal or rubber components for trace-element sampling.
Microbiological analyses: Collect samples for microbiological analyses using equipment and techniques described in NFM 7.

steps.label: Visually inspect the stream cross section and document flow and contamination features

constraints.text: Visually inspect the stream from bank to bank and longitudinally, observing velocity, width, and depth distribution, and apparent distribution of sediment and aquatic biota along the cross section.
Note and document the location of stagnant water, eddies, backwater, reverse flows, areas of faster than normal flow, and piers or other features along the cross section.

steps.label: Determine stream width and record cross-sectional field measurements

constraints.text: Determine stream width from a tagline or from distance markings on a bridge railing or cableway.
At sites with little sampling history, measure and record the cross-sectional variation of field measurements (such as specific electrical conductance, pH, temperature, and dissolved oxygen).
Review the magnitude of the variations along the cross section.

steps.label: Determine the equal-width increment count and locate the first sampling vertical

constraints.text: To obtain the number of increments, divide the stream width by the increment width.
The number of increments must be a whole number.
Increment width is based on study objectives, variation in field measurements and flow, and stream-channel characteristics along the cross section.
Collect the subsample at the center of each equal-width increment (the vertical).
If the subsample does not represent the mean value for that increment, decrease the increment width until the mean value for the increment is represented.
Locate the first sampling vertical at a distance of one-half of the selected increment width from the edge of the water.
Locate all the other verticals at the center of each remaining equal-width increment along the cross section.
Even if streamflow is divided, as in a braided channel, equal-width increments must be identical from channel to channel, and the same constant transit rate must be used at each vertical.
Make slight adjustments to sampling locations, if necessary, to avoid sampling where the flow is affected by a pier or other obstruction.
Isokinetic sampling is required for the EWI method. Use isokinetic, depth-integrating sampling equipment (NFM 2.1.1.A).
Do not use EWI when stream velocities are less than the minimum velocity required for the isokinetic sampler selected (1.5 ft/s for the bottle sampler; 3 ft/s for the bag sampler).
Be sure that the field effort is adequately staffed and equipped. Check QC requirements before departing—QC samples require additional equipment and supplies.

quality.review_notes: Second-pass review. Added S4 (stream width measurement and cross-sectional field measurements, Step 2b-2c) missing from draft. Heavily expanded S2 constraints (C3-C7) splitting selection rules by analyte type with explicit prohibitions. Expanded S5 from 2 to 9 constraints capturing whole-number requirement, mean-value backoff rule, location rules for first and subsequent verticals, braided-channel rule, obstruction adjustment. Procedure-level constraints added (C22-C24) for isokinetic-sampling precondition, minimum-velocity prohibition, and QC pre-departure requirement. Total: 5 steps, 21 step-attached + 3 procedure-level = 24 constraints (was 4 steps, 6 constraints). Scope kept at Step 1-2 of the EWI method (steps 3-6 cover transit rate, sample collection, processing, and equipment cleaning — out of bounded excerpt). STANDARDS REVIEW FIX: C24 (QC pre-departure staffing/equipment) applies_to extended from [S1] to all 5 steps — pre-departure constraint binds the whole procedure, not just S1.
